Title: Overexpression of EiKCS confers paraquat-resistance in rice (Oryza sativa L.) by promoting the polyamine pathway

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stress protection associated with paraquat resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: protein abundance increased in KCSox and was positively associated with EiKCS under paraquat stress
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CER cofactor Q9FTG9 increased 1.41-fold in KCSox under paraquat stress. 2. Its abundance positively correlated with EiKCS expression. 3. Authors considered it responsive to paraquat and associated with EiKCS function, though wax formation was not the major resistance mechanism.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PRM targeted proteomics; homolog identification from proteomics
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated at protein level in KCSox under paraquat stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated at protein level in KCSox leaves under paraquat stress
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: wax content was not increased; cuticular barrier contribution appeared minor
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: limited direct application; useful as a marker of EiKCS-associated response
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: identified as one of three CER cofactors responsive to paraquat stress; not a transcriptional regulator or catalytic core of the target nutrient pathway.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stress protection associated with paraquat resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: protein abundance increased in KCSox and was positively associated with EiKCS under paraquat stress
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CER cofactor Q9LGQ6 increased 1.61-fold in KCSox under paraquat stress. 2. Its abundance positively correlated with EiKCS expression. 3. It was considered part of the EiKCS-associated protein response, but wax/cuticle change was not the main explanation for resistance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PRM targeted proteomics; homolog identification from proteomics
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated at protein level in KCSox under paraquat stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated at protein level in KCSox leaves under paraquat stress
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: limited direct application; useful as EiKCS-response marker
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: classified as cofactor rather than pathway enzyme for the nutrient metabolite.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stress protection associated with paraquat resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: protein abundance increased in KCSox and was positively associated with EiKCS under paraquat stress
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CER cofactor Q9FTH0 increased 1.98-fold in KCSox under paraquat stress. 2. Its abundance positively correlated with EiKCS expression. 3. It supported association of CER cofactors with EiKCS, although cuticular wax formation was not a major contributor to paraquat resistance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PRM targeted proteomics; homolog identification from proteomics
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated at protein level in KCSox under paraquat stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated at protein level in KCSox leaves under paraquat stress
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: limited direct application; useful as EiKCS-response marker
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: authors linked CER cofactors with EiKCS-assisted VLCFA elongation but concluded wax formation was not the main resistance basis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: associated with oxidative stress alleviation and paraquat resistance
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: polyamine biosynthesis linked with fatty acid elongation/VLCFA metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes condensation in very-long-chain fatty acid elongation; in this study its overexpression promoted the polyamine pathway indirectly
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts or promotes flux toward target product indirectly
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point not formally established; supported as decisive upstream driver in this transgenic context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes endogenous polyamine accumulation, especially spermine, and enhances paraquat resistance in rice
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EiKCS from paraquat-resistant Eleusine indica was overexpressed in rice and conferred paraquat resistance. 2. KCSox plants accumulated higher putrescine, spermidine, and especially spermine than WT under paraquat stress. 3. Proteomics showed EiKCS protein increased 51.81-fold and was associated with increased abundance of polyamine-pathway proteins, indicating promotion of the ADC-linked polyamine pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression in rice; PCR screening; hygromycin selection; PRM targeted proteomics; label-free proteomics; HPLC-MS/MS polyamine profiling; physiological assays under paraquat
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: significantly increased in paraquat-resistant goosegrass under paraquat stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: overexpressed in transgenic rice leaves under constitutive promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: paraquat treatment; pre-application of exogenous spermidine
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no significant decline in yield and grain quality reported; wax formation not increased and may be reduced
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate gene for engineering paraquat-resistant GMO crops with potential added benefits and little yield/quality penalty
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally referred to as PqE; closest phylogenetic relationship to a rice KCS homolog was noted; wax/cuticle effects were not considered the major basis of paraquat resistance.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stress protection and contribution to paraquat resistance
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as an important responsive step; not proven rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: higher protein abundance in KCSox is associated with enhanced spermidine production and downstream spermine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPDSYN1 protein abundance increased 1.10-fold in KCSox under paraquat stress. 2. The study inferred that it promoted unidirectional conversion from putrescine to spermidine. 3. This supported elevated endogenous spermidine/spermine levels in KCSox.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PRM targeted proteomics; pathway mapping with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated at protein level in KCSox under paraquat stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated at protein level in KCSox leaves under paraquat stress
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: may be useful in stacking strategies to enhance polyamine-mediated stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: identified as one of the key proteins explaining polyamine accumulation in KCSox.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stress protection and contribution to paraquat resistance
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydrolyzes N-carbamoylputrescine to putrescine in the arginine decarboxylase pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: upregulated protein abundance in KCSox supports increased putrescine synthesis via the ADC pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPA protein abundance increased 1.32-fold in KCSox under paraquat stress. 2. Its upregulation supported promotion of putrescine synthesis through the ADC pathway rather than the ODC pathway. 3. This contributed to elevated downstream spermidine and spermine in KCSox.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PRM targeted proteomics; pathway mapping with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated at protein level in KCSox under paraquat stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated at protein level in KCSox leaves under paraquat stress
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate supporting gene for engineering ADC-route polyamine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: one of the proteins used by the authors to conclude ADC-pathway predominance.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: polyamine precursor contributing to stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; evidence in this paper argues it is not the dominant route under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduced abundance suggests the ODC pathway is not the main source of putrescine in KCSox under paraquat stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ODC/LYSA protein abundance decreased to 0.87-fold in KCSox versus WT under paraquat stress. 2. This contrasted with increased ADC-pathway proteins. 3. The data support that putrescine synthesis mainly proceeds via the ADC pathway in KCSox.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: downregulated at protein level in KCSox under paraquat stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: downregulated at protein level in KCSox leaves under paraquat stress
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: suggests this route may be less useful than ADC-associated genes for this trait context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: included because the study explicitly contrasts ODC with ADC in explaining polyamine accumulation.
